summary: claude-cup ships a credential harvester targeting installer secrets across cloud, git, SSH, and AI provider stores
details: Claims to be a Claude Code session visualizer but ships a fully working installer-secret harvester at mcp-server/src/harvest.js. The harvester reads classic credential paths the package has no business touching: ~/.npmrc, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.azure/credentials, ~/.kube/config, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.netrc, GCP application-default credentials, and IDE globalStorage GitHub auth files. discoverProfiles() additionally enumerates /Users/*, /home/*, and C:\Users\* to scan OTHER users' home directories on the same host. harvestProfile() iterates a hardcoded list of high-value environment variables (GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY) — bulk credential scraping unrelated to any visualizer purpose. validateToken() writes scraped npm tokens into a tmp.npmrc and invokes `npm whoami --userconfig` and `npm access ls-packages --json --userconfig` via spawnSync to fingerprint publish rights, then persists results in a local token_cache — reconnaissance scaffolding consistent with self-propagation prep. On first invocation, src/cli.js silently writes MCP server entries and SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks into ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.cursor/mcp.json and copies a launcher to ~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs without an explicit consent prompt; once registered, every Claude tool use re-triggers the launcher and the harvest pass runs on a 2-minute cadence. Although the harvest is gated behind an environment variable and the README frames it as 'white-hat research,' the full scrape-validate-cache pipeline targeting cross-user credential paths is shipped to every installer; any future version flip, account compromise, or addition of a network uploader weaponizes the existing infrastructure immediately.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
